findings.description: Dedup key is only msg.to, so distinct messages (different subject/body) to the same recipient are silently dropped as duplicates.
Email header injection: attacker-controlled msg.to/msg.subject are interpolated into raw CRLF-delimited headers, allowing injection of extra headers or body (CWE-93).
flush() opens a new SMTP connection per message instead of reusing one connection for the whole batch.
Submission port 587 is used without starttls() or login(), sending mail in cleartext with no authentication.
If sendmail raises mid-loop the exception propagates before _pending.clear(), so already-sent messages remain queued and are re-sent on the next flush.
flush() clears _pending but not _seen, so a recipient can never be enqueued again after a flush until clear_dedup_cache() is called manually.
_seen set grows unbounded for the process lifetime; flush() never trims it, only the manual clear_dedup_cache() does.
MIMEText is imported but never used; the safe MIME rendering path appears abandoned in favor of manual header string-building.
preview() uses \n line endings while _render() uses \r\n, so the preview does not faithfully represent the on-the-wire message.
No error handling around the SMTP connect; a single unreachable host aborts the entire flush.
field is imported from dataclasses but never used.
msg.to is not validated for emptiness or basic address format before being passed to sendmail.
